textEn: By the star when it sets,
Your companion [i.e., the Prophet] has neither strayed nor is he deluded.
textTranslit: Maa dalla saahibukum wa maa ghawaa

textEn: Nor does he speak out of his own desire.
It is but a revelation sent down [to him].
He was taught by [an angel of] mighty power,
and of great perfection, who manifested himself [in his true form]
when he was on the higher horizon.
Then he approached [the Prophet] and descended,
until he was only two bow-lengths away or even closer.
Then he revealed to His slave what he had to reveal.
The [Prophet’s] heart did not deny what he saw.
textTranslit: Maa kazabal fu'aadu maa ra aa

textEn: Do you dispute with him concerning what he saw?
He certainly saw him a second time
textTranslit: Wa laqad ra aahu nazlatan ukhraa

textEn: at the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary [in the seventh heaven]
near which is the Garden of Abode,
when the Lote Tree was overwhelmed with [divine] splendors.
His gaze did not wander, nor did it look beyond the limit.
textTranslit: Maa zaaghal basaru wa maa taghaa

textAr: لَقَدْ رَأَىٰ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِ ٱلْكُبْرَىٰٓ
textEn: He certainly saw some of the greatest signs of his Lord.
textTranslit: Laqad ra aa min aayaati Rabbihil kubraaa

textEn: Have you [O disbelievers] thought about [the idols of] Lāt and ‘Uzzā,
and Manāt, the third one as well?
Do you choose for yourselves males and for Him females?
That is then an unfair division!

textAr: إِنْ هِىَ إِلَّآ أَسْمَآءٌ سَمَّيْتُمُوهَآ أَنتُمْ وَءَابَآؤُكُم مَّآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَا مِن سُلْطَـٰنٍ ۚ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ وَمَا تَهْوَى ٱلْأَنفُسُ ۖ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَهُم مِّن رَّبِّهِمُ ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ
textEn: These [idols] are no more than names you and your forefathers have made up, for which Allah has not sent down any authority. They follow nothing but conjecture and what their souls desire, although there has come to them guidance from their Lord.
textTranslit: In hiya illaaa asmaaa'un sammaitumoohaaa antum wa aabaaa'ukum maaa anzalal laahu bihaa min sultaan; inyyattabi'oona illaz zanna wa maa tahwal anfusu wa laqad jaaa'ahum mir Rabbihimul hudaa

textEn: Is it that man can get whatever he wishes for?
textTranslit: Am lil insaani maa taman naa

textEn: Rather, to Allah belongs the Hereafter and this world.

textAr: ۞ وَكَم مِّن مَّلَكٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ لَا تُغْنِى شَفَـٰعَتُهُمْ شَيْـًٔا إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِ أَن يَأْذَنَ ٱللَّهُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَرْضَىٰٓ
textEn: How many angels there are in the heavens whose intercession will be of no avail except after Allah’s permission to whom He wills and pleases.
textTranslit: Wa kam mim malakin fissamaawaati laa tughnee shafaa'atuhum shai'an illaa mim ba'di anyyaazanal laahu limany yashaaa'u wa yardaa

textAr: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ لَيُسَمُّونَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ تَسْمِيَةَ ٱلْأُنثَىٰ
textEn: Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter give the angels female names.
textTranslit: innal lazeena laa yu'minoona bil aakhirati la yusammoonal malaaa'ikata tasmiyatal unsaa

textAr: وَمَا لَهُم بِهِۦ مِنْ عِلْمٍ ۖ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ ۖ وَإِنَّ ٱلظَّنَّ لَا يُغْنِى مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ شَيْـًٔا
textEn: They have no knowledge about it. They only follow conjecture, but conjecture is of no avail against the truth.
textTranslit: Wa maa lahum bihee min 'ilmin iny yattabi'oona illaz zanna wa innaz zanna laa yughnee minal haqqi shai'aa

textAr: فَأَعْرِضْ عَن مَّن تَوَلَّىٰ عَن ذِكْرِنَا وَلَمْ يُرِدْ إِلَّا ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا
textEn: So pay no heed to those who turn away from Our message and who only seek the life of this world.
textTranslit: Fa a'rid 'am man tawallaa 'an zikrinaa wa lam yurid illal hayaatad dunyaa

textAr: ذَٰلِكَ مَبْلَغُهُم مِّنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ
textEn: That is the limit of their knowledge. Indeed, your Lord knows best who has strayed from His Way, and He knows best who is guided.
textTranslit: Zalika mablaghuhum minal 'ilm; inna rabbaka huwa a'lamu biman dalla 'an sabee lihee wa huwa a'lamu bimanih tadaa

textAr: وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ لِيَجْزِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسَـٰٓـُٔوا۟ بِمَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَيَجْزِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا۟ بِٱلْحُسْنَى
textEn: To Allah belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is on earth, so that He may recompense the evildoers for what they did, and reward those who do good with the best reward,
textTranslit: Wa lillaahi maa fis samaawaati wa maa fil ardi liyajziyal lazeena asaaa'oo bimaa 'amiloo wa yajziyal lazeena ahsanoo bilhusnaa

textAr: ٱلَّذِينَ يَجْتَنِبُونَ كَبَـٰٓئِرَ ٱلْإِثْمِ وَٱلْفَوَٰحِشَ إِلَّا ٱللَّمَمَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ وَٰسِعُ ٱلْمَغْفِرَةِ ۚ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِكُمْ إِذْ أَنشَأَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَإِذْ أَنتُمْ أَجِنَّةٌ فِى بُطُونِ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ ۖ فَلَا تُزَكُّوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمْ ۖ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنِ ٱتَّقَىٰٓ
textEn: those who avoid major sins and shameful acts, except for minor lapses. Indeed, your Lord is ample in forgiveness. He knows you best, as He created you from the earth and when you were fetuses in the wombs of your mothers. So do not claim purity for yourselves, for He knows best who is truly righteous.
textTranslit: Allazeena yajtaniboona kabaaa'iral ismi walfawaa hisha illal lamam; inna rabbaka waasi'ul maghfirah; huwa a'lamu bikum iz ansha akum minal ardi wa iz antum ajinnatun fee butooni umma haatikum falaa tuzakkooo anfusakum huwa a'lamu bimanit taqaa

textEn: Have you seen the one who turned away [from Islam],
who gave a little, and then stopped?
Does he have knowledge of the unseen, so he has insight?

textAr: أَمْ لَمْ يُنَبَّأْ بِمَا فِى صُحُفِ مُوسَىٰ
textEn: Or has he not been informed of what is in the Scriptures of Moses,
textTranslit: Am lam yunabbaa bimaa fee suhuhfi Moosa

textEn: and of Abraham, who fulfilled his duty?
That no bearer of burden will bear the burden of another,

textAr: وَأَن لَّيْسَ لِلْإِنسَـٰنِ إِلَّا مَا سَعَىٰ
textEn: and that man will not receive except what he strives for,
textTranslit: Wa al laisa lil insaani illaa maa sa'aa

textEn: and that his efforts will be examined,
then he will be recompensed in full,
and that to your Lord is the final return,
and that He is the One Who makes people laugh and weep,
textTranslit: Wa annahoo huwa adhaka wa abkaa

textEn: and that He is the One Who causes death and gives life,
textTranslit: Wa annahoo huwa amaata wa ahyaa

textEn: and that He created the pairs, male and female,
textTranslit: Wa annahoo khalaqaz zawjainiz zakara wal unsaa

textEn: from a drop of sperm when it is emitted,
and it is upon Him to bring about the second creation.
And He is the One Who enriches and bestows wealth,
textTranslit: Wa annahoo huwa aghnaa wa aqnaa

textEn: and that He is the Lord of Sirius.
And that He destroyed the earlier ‘Ād,
and Thamūd, sparing none,

textAr: وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ مِّن قَبْلُ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ هُمْ أَظْلَمَ وَأَطْغَىٰ
textEn: and [He destroyed] the people of Noah before them; they were indeed more wicked and more rebellious.
textTranslit: Wa qawma Noohim min qablu innahum kaanoo hum azlama wa atghaa

textEn: And He toppled the cities upside down,
and they were utterly covered [by stones].
Then which of the favors of your Lord do you doubt?
This [Prophet] is a warner like the earlier warners.
The imminent Hour has drawn near,

textAr: لَيْسَ لَهَا مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ كَاشِفَةٌ
textEn: none but Allah can disclose it.
textTranslit: Laisa lahaa min doonil laahi kaashifah

textEn: Do you find this discourse astonishing,
and you laugh [at it] and not weep,
while heedlessly lost in amusement?
So prostrate to Allah and worship [Him].
